title: Reasons for determination — July 3, 2026
summary: Canadian manufacturers of steel fasteners face new anti-dumping duties on imports from Vietnam, increasing their raw material costs and improving their price competitiveness against foreign competitors.

title: Finding — June 26, 2026
summary: Canada's trade tribunal ruled that imported steel fasteners harm domestic producers, meaning Canadian fastener makers may gain tariff protection against cheaper foreign competition starting soon.

title: Reasons for determination — June 24, 2026
summary: Canadian manufacturers and importers of fasteners face new anti-dumping duties on Chinese-origin products after the CITT confirmed Chinese competitors are selling below cost.

title: Determination — June 18, 2026
summary: Canada's import tariff on steel fasteners from most countries is rising to 25%, making these essential components more expensive for manufacturers using them in production.

title: Determination — June 9, 2026
summary: Canadian importers of frozen strawberries from the U.S. face new anti-dumping duties after the CITT found American producers were selling below cost.

title: Reasons for determination — June 1, 2026
summary: Canadian companies importing steel products must now meet stricter domestic content requirements or face higher tariffs, effective immediately.

title: Public hearing — May 25, 2026
summary: The Canadian International Trade Tribunal is holding a public hearing on May 25, 2026, where businesses can present evidence and arguments in a trade dispute case.

title: Determination — May 15, 2026
summary: Canada's International Trade Tribunal ruled that imported steel fasteners harm domestic producers, triggering new tariffs that will increase costs for Canadian manufacturers using these parts.
